name: kuksa-client

metadata.name: kuksa-client
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# KUKSA Python SDK
![kuksa.val Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/main/docs/pictures/logo.png)

KUKSA Python Client and SDK is a part of the open source project [Eclipse KUKSA](https://www.eclipse.org/kuksa/).
More about Eclipse KUKSA can be found in the [repository](https://github.com/eclipse/kuksa.val).

## Introduction

KUKSA Python SDK provides both a command-line interface (CLI) and a standalone library to interact with either
[KUKSA Server](https://github.com/eclipse/kuksa.val/tree/master/kuksa-val-server) or
[KUKSA Databroker](https://github.com/eclipse/kuksa.val/tree/master/kuksa_databroker).


## Building and Installing the KUKSA Python SDK

The fastest way to start using KUKSA Python SDK is to install a pre-built version from pypi.org:

```console
pip install kuksa-client
```

If you want to install from sources instead see the KUKSA Python SDK [Build documentation](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/docs/building.md).

## Using the Command Line Interface (CLI)

After you have installed the kuksa-client package via pip you can run the test client CLI directly by executing:

```console
kuksa-client
```

With default CLI arguments, the client will try to connect to a local Databroker, e.g. a server supporting the `kuksa.val.v1` protocol without using TLS. This is equivalent to executing

```console
kuksa-client grpc://127.0.0.1:55555
```

More details on how to use the CLI is available in the KUKSA Python SDK [CLI documentation](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/docs/cli.md)

## Using Docker for the CLI

The KUKSA Python SDK CLI is available as a [prebuilt docker container](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/pkgs/container/kuksa-python-sdk%2Fkuksa-client).
For more details see the KUKSA Python SDK [Docker documentation](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/docs/docker.md).

## Using KUKSA Python SDK as library

The KUKSA Python SDK provides three APIS for connecting and communicating with [KUKSA Server](https://github.com/eclipse/kuksa.val/tree/master/kuksa-val-server)
and [KUKSA Databroker](https://github.com/eclipse/kuksa.val/tree/master/kuksa_databroker).
For more details see the KUKSA Python SDK [Library documentation](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/docs/library.md).

## Contributing to KUKSA Python SDK

The KUKSA project welcomes contributions.

See the KUKSA Python SDK [Contribition document](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for formal requirements.

## Development and Troubleshooting

For information on tools useful for KUKSA Python SDK development environment and help on troubleshooting frequent problems please visit
the KUKSA Python SDK [development and troubleshooting documentation](https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-python-sdk/blob/main/docs/development_troubleshoot.md).

classes.name: kuksa.val.v1.val_pb2_grpc.VALStub

methods.name: kuksa.val.v1.val_pb2_grpc.VALStub.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    channel: A grpc.Channel.

Note on authorization:
Tokens (auth-token or auth-uuid) are sent as (GRPC / http2) metadata.

The auth-token is a JWT compliant token as the examples found here:
https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokertree/master/certificates/jwt

See also https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokerblob/master/doc/jwt.md

Upon reception of auth-token, server shall generate an auth-uuid in metadata
that the client can use instead of auth-token in subsequent calls.

Returns a stream of notifications.

InvalidArgument is returned if the request is malformed.

methods.name: kuksa.val.v1.val_pb2_grpc.VALServicer.GetServerInfo
documentation:
Shall return information that allows the client to determine
what server/server implementation/version it is talking to
eg. kuksa-databroker 0.5.1

Note on authorization:
Tokens (auth-token or auth-uuid) are sent as (GRPC / http2) metadata.

The auth-token is a JWT compliant token as the examples found here:
https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokertree/master/certificates/jwt

See also https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokerblob/master/doc/jwt.md

Upon reception of auth-token, server shall generate an auth-uuid in metadata
that the client can use instead of auth-token in subsequent calls.

classes.name: kuksa.val.v1.val_pb2_grpc.VAL
documentation:
Note on authorization:
Tokens (auth-token or auth-uuid) are sent as (GRPC / http2) metadata.

The auth-token is a JWT compliant token as the examples found here:
https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokertree/master/certificates/jwt

See also https://github.com/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databrokerblob/master/doc/jwt.md

Upon reception of auth-token, server shall generate an auth-uuid in metadata
that the client can use instead of auth-token in subsequent calls.
This class is part of an EXPERIMENTAL API.

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_array_parse_no_quote
documentation: No need for quotes just because you have a blank
Client rules:
For simple strings like abd it is optional to quote them ("abc") or not (abc)
Quotes are needed if you have commas ("ab, c")
If you have duoble quotes in strings you must escape them
Note that KUKSA Server has different rules, there the payload must be valid JSON,
so not all tests shown below are recommended as they cannot be used for KUKSA Server

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_array_parse_no_inside_quote
documentation: Quotes are OK

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_array_parse_no_inside_quote_single
documentation: Quotes are OK

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_array_square
documentation: No problem having square brackets as part of strings

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_array_empty_string_not_quoted
documentation: In this case the first item is ignored

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_double_comma
documentation: In this case the middle item is ignored

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_quotes_in_string_values
documentation: Escaped double quotes, so in total 4 items

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_quotes_in_string_values_2
documentation: Double quotes in double quotes so in total three values

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_int_quote
documentation: Quoting individual int values is not allowed

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_cast_str
documentation: Unquoted quotation marks shall be removed, quoted kept without quotes

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_from_message_none
documentation: There shall always be a value

functions.name: tests.test_datapoint.test_from_message_time
documentation:
Make sure that we can handle values out of range (by discarding them)
gRPC supports year up to including 9999, so any date in year 10000 or later shall
result in that None is returned

documentation: Constructor

methods.name: kuksa_client.KuksaClientThread.checkConnection
documentation:
Check if thread has established a connection to the broker/server.
Note that this method does not indicate the current state of the connection,
This method may return True even if the broker/server currently is not reachable.
Deprecated - Use connection_established() instead"
PEP 702 deprecated decorator available first in Python 3.13

methods.name: kuksa_client.KuksaClientThread.connection_established
documentation:
Check if thread has established a connection to the broker/server.
Note that this method does not indicate the current state of the connection,
This method may return True even if the broker/server currently is not reachable.

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.check_connected_async
documentation:
Decorator to verify that there is a connection before calling underlying method
For generator methods use check_connected_async_iter

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.check_connected_async_iter
documentation: Decorator for generator methods to verify that there is a connection before calling underlying method

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.get_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    current_values = await client.get_current_values([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ])
    speed_value = current_values['Vehicle.Speed'].value

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.get_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    target_values = await client.get_target_values([
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ])
    is_abs_to_become_active = target_values['Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive'].value

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.get_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    metadata = await client.get_metadata([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ], MetadataField.UNIT)
    speed_unit = metadata['Vehicle.Speed'].unit

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.set_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    await client.set_current_values({
        'Vehicle.Speed': Datapoint(42),
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive': Datapoint(False),
    })

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.set_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    await client.set_target_values({'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive': Datapoint(True)})

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.set_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    await client.set_metadata({
        'Vehicle.Cabin.Door.Row1.Left.Shade.Position': Metadata(data_type=DataType.FLOAT),
    })

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.subscribe_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    async for updates in client.subscribe_current_values([
        'Vehicle.Speed', 'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, dp in updates.items():
            print(f"Current value for {path} is now: {dp.value}")

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.subscribe_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    async for updates in client.subscribe_target_values([
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, dp in updates.items():
            print(f"Target value for {path} is now: {dp.value}")

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.aio.VSSClient.subscribe_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    async for updates in client.subscribe_metadata([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, md in updates.items():
            print(f"Metadata for {path} are now: {md.to_dict()}")

methods.name: kuksa_client.__main__.TestClient.connection_established
documentation:
Check if thread has established a connection to the broker/server.
Note that this method does not indicate the current state of the connection,
This method may return True even if the broker/server currently is not reachable.

to_message/from_message aligned to use None rather than empty list for

documentation: pylint: enable=too-many-branches

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.Datapoint.from_message
documentation: Return internal Datapoint representation or None on error

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.Datapoint.cast_array_values
documentation:
Parses array input and cast individual values to wanted type.
Note that input value to this function is not the same as given if you use kuksa-client command line
as parts (e.g. surrounding quotes) are removed by shell, and then do_setValue also do some magic.

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.Datapoint.cast_str
documentation:
Strip based on the following rules.
- Leading/Trailing blanks are removed
- If there are single or double quote at both start and end they are removed
- Finally any quote escapes are removed

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.get_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    current_values = client.get_current_values([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ])
    speed_value = current_values['Vehicle.Speed'].value

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.get_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    target_values = client.get_target_values([
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ])
    is_abs_to_become_active = target_values['Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive'].value

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.get_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    metadata = client.get_metadata([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ], MetadataField.UNIT)
    speed_unit = metadata['Vehicle.Speed'].unit

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.set_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    client.set_current_values({
        'Vehicle.Speed': Datapoint(42),
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive': Datapoint(False),
    })

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.set_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    client.set_target_values(
        {'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive': Datapoint(True)})

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.set_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    client.set_metadata({
        'Vehicle.Cabin.Door.Row1.Left.Shade.Position': Metadata(data_type=DataType.FLOAT),
    })

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.subscribe_current_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    for updates in client.subscribe_current_values([
        'Vehicle.Speed', 'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, dp in updates.items():
            print(f"Current value for {path} is now: {dp.value}")

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.subscribe_target_values
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    async for updates in client.subscribe_target_values([
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, dp in updates.items():
            print(f"Target value for {path} is now: {dp.value}")

methods.name: kuksa_client.grpc.VSSClient.subscribe_metadata
documentation:
Parameters:
    rpc_kwargs
        grpc.*MultiCallable kwargs e.g. timeout, metadata, credentials.
Example:
    for updates in client.subscribe_metadata([
        'Vehicle.Speed',
        'Vehicle.ADAS.ABS.IsActive',
    ]):
        for path, md in updates.items():
            print(f"Metadata for {path} are now: {md.to_dict()}")
